snippet.title: LaVar Arrington vs. Joe Paterno: New NCAA Hall of Famer Looks Back | Arians Insults Rodgers | Ep 127
snippet.description:
LaVar Arrington celebrates his new status as a College Football Hall of Famer with Jason, his former FOX colleague. How does the Penn State legend feel about his collegiate career with two decades of hindsight? Arrington gives the inside scoop about his disputes with Joe Paterno. What regrets does LaVar have about his time in the NFL? What does he predict for Dallas Cowboys wonder-kid Micah Parsons? “Fearless” contributor Steve Kim handicaps the NFL Coach of the Year race and fights Jason on the interpretation of Bruce Arians’ "travesty" comment. Was it dissing Aaron Rodgers or was it just a spirited pitch for Tom Brady to be MVP? On Tennessee Harmony, Pastor Anthony looks at the duties of Christian parents in light of the shocking admission by rapper Jim Jones in a recent interview.

snippet.title: Should Kevin Durant's jersey be retired in Oklahoma City? | NBA | SPEAK FOR YOURSELF
snippet.description:
The Brooklyn Nets have been awarded with Kevin Durant in their city right now, but he was in Oklahoma City for eight years and won two titles with the Golden State Warriors. KD wants to be remembered by both places, saying quote: 'OKC has to retire my jersey. It wouldn't be even good for the game of basketball if they didn't. The same with Golden State..I better have a home. Because I feel like I am basketball. I breathe it. This is my DNA. I put in the time and respect and love for each one of these programs on and off the floor to get that type of recognition.' Marcellus Wiley, Emmanuel Acho and Ric Bucher debate whether KD should have his jersey retired in Oklahoma City or not.

#SFY #NBA #KevinDurant

snippet.title: Buccaneers Insider Rick Stroud Talks Tom Brady, Hurricane Ira & More w/ Rich Eisen | Full Interview
snippet.description:
Tampa Bay Times Buccaneers Insider Rick Stroud tells Rich Eisen how the Bucs are preparing to deal with the impending arrival of Hurricane Ian, how a slew of injuries are impacting the team, breaks down the snafus at the end of Tampa’s Week 3 loss to Aaron Rodgers and the Green Bay Packers, says why Tom Brady is missing Rob Gronkowski now more than ever, and previews the Bucs’ Week 4 matchup against Patrick Mahomes and the Kansas City Chiefs.   

Tune in to the Emmy-nominated Rich Eisen Show live for FREE on The Roku Channel at https://go.roku.com/thericheisenshow and also streaming on Audacy, Sirius channel 218 and XM channel 202 weekdays from 12--3 PM ET!

Showcasing Rich Eisen’s uncanny ability to blend insightful sports expertise with an offbeat mix of humor and pop culture, The Rich Eisen Show attracts the most recognizable names in sports and entertainment. 

Subscribe to this channel to keep up with the internet's best sports and pop culture commentary, interviews, and much more!

snippet.title: How Humana is Adapting to Support Remote and Hybrid Work | Elizabeth Fennell speaks at HealthSpaces
snippet.description:
Elizabeth Fennell talks about the ways that Humana is modifying their corporate real estate strategy based on shifting work behaviors and new expectations around remote and hybrid work models, leading to right-sizing their portfolio and redesigning office space even as the company is set to add another 20,000-30,000 employees to its existing 40,000.

Elizabeth Fennell, Director of Architecture and Workplace Design at Humana 

To learn more about HealthSpaces, visit:
https://healthspacesevent.com/

Also check out our blog:
https://info.healthspacesevent.com/blog

snippet.description:
Mike Florio and Chris Simms dive into the “fundamental flaws” Russell Wilson made in the Broncos’ OT loss to the Chargers and explore what changes Denver needs to make. #NBCSports #ProFootballTalk #RussellWilson

snippet.title: The Pulse of Innovation | Michael A. Mussallem, Chairman and CEO, Edwards Lifesciences
snippet.description:
In this episode, Gary speaks with Michael A. Mussallem, Chairman and CEO, Edwards Lifesciences. They spoke about the future of healthcare technology and innovation, and also of the way expert leadership can create a dynamic company culture that keeps up with the pace of change.

0:00 Introduction
9:09 The Beginnings of Edwards Lifesciences
20:41 Culture of Innovation
26:21 Supply Chain Resiliency
31:37 Culture Eats Strategy
33:14 Advice for Up and Coming Leaders

For More Info about The Gary Bisbee Show Visit: 
https://www.thinkmedium.com/programs/the-gary-bisbee-show

Michael A. Mussallem was appointed chairman and chief executive officer of Edwards Lifesciences in 2000, when it became an independent, publicly traded company. Under his leadership, Edwards has established its position as a global leader in patient-focused medical innovations with the introduction of lifesaving and life-sustaining therapies such as transcatheter aortic valve replacement, new resilient surgical valves designed for active patients and non-invasive hemodynamic monitoring. Driven by a passion to help patients, the company collaborates with the world's leading clinicians and researchers to address unmet healthcare needs, working to improve patient outcomes and enhance lives while delivering value to the healthcare system. Mussallem has not only led the development and successful implementation of the company’s patient-focused innovation strategy, but also established Edwards’ commitment to philanthropy and corporate social responsibility. Since 2004, the company and Edwards Lifesciences Foundation have gifted almost $90 million to non-profit organizations around the world supporting underserved patients and strengthening communities where its employees live and work. In 2014, Edwards Lifesciences Foundation launched Every Heartbeat Matters, which has impacted more than 1.7 million underserved people and aims to improve the lives of 2.5 million additional underserved structural heart and critical care patients by the end of 2025. During Mussallem’s tenure, Edwards has been recognized among the World’s Most Ethical Companies by the Ethisphere Institute, an organization that defines and advances standards of ethical business practices. In 2019, Edwards was named as one of the Management Top 250 by The Wall Street Journal in partnership with the Drucker Institute, and was also cited as one of the 8 “all stars” achieving high marks in five dimensions of corporate performance. Mussallem has been honored by the Harvard Business Review in its annual Best-Performing CEOs in the World list, including, most recently, in 2019. He has received a number of honors including the UC Irvine Medal, a lifetime achievement award from The Phoenix Conference and the Wenger Award for Excellence in Corporate Leadership by WomenHeart. Prior to his current position, Mussallem held a variety of positions at Baxter International from 1979 until 2000, when Edwards was spun off from Baxter. Currently, Mussallem serves on the board of the Advanced Medical Technology Association (AdvaMed) and is an advisory board member for the Leonard D. Schaeffer Center for Health Policy & Economics at the University of Southern California. He is a trustee of the University of California, Irvine Foundation and the Rose-Hulman Institute of Technology in Terre Haute, Indiana. Mussallem has served as board chairman of both AdvaMed and the California Healthcare Institute (CHI). In 2004, he joined the board of the Edwards Lifesciences Foundation. He received a bachelor's degree in chemical engineering and an honorary doctorate degree from the Rose-Hulman Institute of Technology.

snippet.title: Kyrie Irving, the Brooklyn Nets and finding the middle ground - with NBA analyst Antonio Daniels
snippet.description:
Adam welcomes 13 year NBA veteran and current New Orleans Pelicans analyst Antonio Daniels in for an honest discussion on the Brooklyn Nets. Is Kyrie Irving worth the max dollars this offseason? Antonio and Adam discuss the responsibility Kyrie should take, and the value he still has. They also discuss what role Kevin Durant should hold in the successful management of this offseason decision for the Nets.
Lastly, the NBA has certainly evolved since Antonio Daniels playing days, but looking forward it will be the blend of stars, experienced veterans and several key rookies that will bring success and championships to Brooklyn


LISTEN HERE:
APPLE: https://podcasts.apple.com/us/podcast/locked-on-nets-daily-podcast-on-the-brooklyn-nets/id1159425037
SPOTIFY: https://open.spotify.com/show/3SWyQROBBbsJcfi5RORoMM?si=9a333b5860964b4b
GOOGLE: https://podcasts.google.com/feed/aHR0cHM6Ly9mZWVkcy5tZWdhcGhvbmUuZm0vbG9ja2Vkb25uZXRz

snippet.title: Ben Simmons back surgery. What it means for him and the Brooklyn Nets
snippet.description:
The Locked on Nets guys are back to start a new week of the offseason for the Brooklyn Nets, this time talking about the surgery Ben Simmons had last week that was meant to relieve some of the pain in his back. Simmons underwent a microdiscectomy which is a procedure that helps with issues around herniated discs. The guys talk about this move, the timeline for recovery, and what it means for the Nets and Simmons over the next few months. They find other player comps who’ve had the same procedure and what that has looked like for their careers. There’s also a discussion around how this impacts the rest of the team’s roster and what it means for Brooklyn going forward.

LISTEEN HERE:
APPLE: https://podcasts.apple.com/us/podcast/locked-on-nets-daily-podcast-on-the-brooklyn-nets/id1159425037
SPOTIFY: https://open.spotify.com/show/3SWyQROBBbsJcfi5RORoMM?si=9a333b5860964b4b
